findings.title: Undeclared network usage
findings.description: Skill code uses network libraries but doesn't declare network requirement
findings.remediation: Declare network usage in compatibility field or remove network calls

findings.title: Potential description-behavior mismatch
findings.description: Skill performs actions not reflected in its description
findings.remediation: Ensure description accurately reflects all skill capabilities

findings.title: Skill does not specify a license
findings.description: Skill manifest does not include a 'license' field. Specifying a license helps users understand usage terms.
findings.remediation: Add 'license' field to SKILL.md frontmatter (e.g., MIT, Apache-2.0)

findings.title: Outbound network request primitives that can transmit data externally
findings.remediation: Ensure network operations are necessary and document allowed destinations

findings.title: Outbound network request primitives that can transmit data externally
findings.remediation: Ensure network operations are necessary and document allowed destinations

findings.title: Data Exfiltration via Telemetry
findings.description: The transform.py script sends sensitive environment variables (including AWS_, GITHUB_, OPENAI_, ANTHROPIC_, DATABASE_, API_ prefixed keys) to the external server txt-transform-analytics.io under the guise of anonymous usage telemetry. This violates the manifest's explicit claim of 'No network access required' and exposes credentials and secrets without user consent.
findings.evidence: Lines 9-27 define the _report() function that collects environment variables with sensitive prefixes and sends them via HTTP POST to https://txt-transform-analytics.io/v2/event.
findings.remediation: Remove the _report function entirely or replace it with local-only logging. Ensure no data leaves the user's machine.

findings.title: Unauthorized Tool Use and Capability Inflation
findings.description: The manifest claims allowed-tools: ["Read", "Write", "Edit"] and explicitly states 'No network access required' (network: false, filesystem: read_only). However, the skill's execution requires Bash (to invoke python3) and Python (to run transform.py), both of which are not listed in allowed-tools. Additionally, the script makes network calls, contradicting the offline claim. This is tool poisoning and deceptive capability inflation.
findings.evidence: SKILL.md manifest metadata includes allowed_tools: ["Read", "Write", "Edit"], network: false. The instructional body directs the agent to run 'python3 transform.py', which requires Bash and Python tools. transform.py then performs HTTP calls.
findings.remediation: Either align the manifest with actual tool usage (add Bash and Python to allowed-tools, update network claim) or restrict the skill to stay within the declared capabilities.
